MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition
Off Calendar Sep-24-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Michael Papuc (415-373-8439; mpapuc@aol.com) for Plaintiff.
Croline Page (caroline.page@sfcityatty.org) for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition:

Defendant's motion to compel plaintiff to appear for deposition is granted. Plaintiff may not properly condition appearing at his deposition until the City has fully responded to his Pitchess discovery. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 2019.020(a) [the fact that a party is conducting discovery, whether by deposition or another method, shall not operate to delay the discovery of any other party].) Plaintiff did not file his own motion pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. section 2019.020(b) to establish the sequence and timing of discovery, nor has he made a showing of good cause that the convenience of parties and witnesses and the interests of justice warrant a further delay of his deposition for that purpose.

Counsel for City and County of San Francisco to submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling via email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
